title: Binary representation is faithful: from_bits ∘ to_bits = id
statement_informal: Writing a natural number in base 2 (least-significant bit first) and reading it back recovers it. to_bits n = (n mod 2) :: to_bits (n/2); from_bits is Horner in base 2. Theorem: from_bits (to_bits fuel n) = n for n <= fuel.

cost_model.notes: to_bits peels one bit per step (n mod 2, n/2); from_bits is base-2 Horner. Round-trip via n = 2*(n/2)+n mod 2 (Nat.div_mod_eq).

references: Positional (base-2) numeral system — the encode/decode round-trip is the faithfulness of binary representation.
